page: Alibaba Cloud ecs.g9it.large is a general-purpose virtual machine instance powered by the KVM hypervisor. It features an x86_64 architecture with an Intel Xeon 6982P-C processor running at a clock speed of 3.2 GHz. The instance is configured with 2 dedicated vCPUs corresponding to 2 physical cores, operating at 1.0 thread per core. This dedicated CPU allocation ensures that compute resources are not shared with other tenants, providing predictable execution.

The instance is equipped with 8.0 GB of system memory, yielding a memory-to-core ratio of 4.0 GB per core. It does not include any local storage, meaning all storage requirements must be met through external network-attached block storage. Network connectivity is supported by a baseline bandwidth of 4 Gbps. No complimentary public IPv4 addresses are bundled with this instance type.

The lack of hyperthreading limits parallel thread execution but guarantees that each vCPU has exclusive access to a physical core's execution resources. The absence of local storage and GPU acceleration makes this instance unsuitable for high-performance storage-intensive databases or machine learning workloads. It is designed for general-purpose workloads, including web serving, application hosting, and lightweight database operations that require consistent CPU performance without massive memory or storage footprints.
description: Alibaba Cloud ecs.g9it.large is a general-purpose instance utilizing the KVM hypervisor and an x86_64 Intel Xeon 6982P-C processor running at 3.2 GHz. It features 2 dedicated vCPUs mapped to 2 physical cores with 1.0 thread per core, ensuring dedicated compute allocation without hyperthreading. The hardware configuration includes 8.0 GB of RAM, providing 4.0 GB per core, and a baseline network bandwidth of 4 Gbps. This instance does not include local storage or GPU accelerators, requiring external block storage for data persistence. The dedicated CPU resources make it suitable for workloads requiring predictable performance, such as web serving, application hosting, and small database deployments, while the lack of local storage and GPUs represents a tradeoff for storage-heavy or accelerated computing tasks.

tagline: A dedicated dual-core Intel Xeon instance with 8 GB RAM and 4 Gbps network bandwidth for predictable general-purpose workloads.
